memory_seeds.text: The Loup-Garou was a wreck when I found her. Mining laser mounts torn off, patch armor over holes that shouldn't exist, half the conduits rerouted through the cargo bay. I made her fly again. She's mine now.

I worked three years in a Corp Charter shipyard. They wanted speed, not safety. Every ship I saw leave that yard had a design flaw I could spot from the gantry. I walked out after the Santiago incident.
The jump drive is held together with spite and good intentions. Don't touch it unless I'm there.
